acceptanceCriteria: A provider that the Claude Code execution layer structurally cannot invoke derives incompatible even when probing is unsupported; probe health never erases static incompatibility.
Unknown still never excludes a candidate and unsupported probing still never derives available.

Availability reasons and documentation describe RIGOR_CODEX_PLUGIN_PRESENT/RIGOR_CODEX_PLUGIN_VERSION as orchestrator-declared channels, not direct plugin observations, in docs/orchestration.md, README.md, and README.ja.md.
The ANTHROPIC_MODEL bound applies to the trimmed value, consistent with boundedVersion.
Only tests introduced by GH-12 are adjusted to the corrected behavior; no pre-existing test is deleted, weakened, or skipped.
Full suite (test:all, bench, plugin validate --strict, git diff --check) passes.
constraints: Remediate only independent-review findings 5, 6, and 9; no other behavior change.
Keep schema files unchanged; the artifact format is not affected.
Preserve the unverified identity boundary and fail-safe unknown semantics.
Do not delete, weaken, or skip pre-existing tests.
stopConditions: a required check fails twice without a new hypothesis
